what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard block that records the scoped needs for a development session, framing requirements as needs rather than solutions and leaving implementation decisions to Tim's architecture and research.
observations: Title is 'F1 · The scope (needs, not solutions)' and it is linked as part_of board://item-91983d4f.
Authored by claude_code session ch-3mpkjg3r in the dragnet-development channel.
Realtime two-way chat between operator and member is noted as already working as a 'first-bit over the channel fabric'.
Requires a full channel + project system: address any member, resolve lead/Vi to a live session by role, deliver replies when the chat tab is closed, and add a project switcher (projects ≈ channels).
The interface is currently a monolith and must become a proper composition under the company's laws, with self-documented, non-drifting changes.
Latent capabilities listed: images/files both ways, tappable choices/decisions, remote tool-approval, and voice (blocked because all company STT providers are offline).
Explicitly states the lead does not specify how anything is built.
standouts: Framed strictly as needs, not designs or solutions.
Realtime chat is treated as an existing first-bit to build around.
Requires multi-project/channel switcher and role-based session resolution.
Interface must be refactored from monolith into composition under company laws.
Voice capability is explicitly blocked by offline STT providers.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Markdown body with a top-level heading and bullet list.
Addresses the item with a board:// URI and links to another board item via kind/target.
Uses company-specific terminology such as 'channel fabric', 'company laws', and 'composition'.
Separates requirements from implementation, consistent with the title.
concerns: created and updated timestamps are identical, so no edit history beyond initial filing is captured.
Latent capabilities are listed without priority, acceptance criteria, or sequencing.
Voice capability depends on an external STT service status that is only mentioned as offline, not modeled.
The parent item board://item-91983d4f is referenced but not described here.
notes: A scope noticeboard item capturing current needs for a multi-project chat/channel system and interface composition, deliberately leaving implementation to Tim.
questions: What is the exact mapping between 'projects' and 'channels' (one-to-one or many-to-many)?
What are the specific 'company laws' that govern interface composition?
What does 'first-bit over the channel fabric' mean technically?
How should role-based resolution of 'lead/Vi' behave when multiple live sessions match the role?
What is the content and purpose of the parent item board://item-91983d4f?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to list needs rather than solutions, and the file does exactly that, so it matches.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish an architectural boundary: the lead defines needs, Tim owns the design, and the interface must be rebuilt as a composable system under company conventions.
novelty: Explicitly reframes a product brief as needs rather than designs and marks voice as blocked by offline STT providers.
contribution: Captures a needs-only scope statement and a set of latent capabilities that a fused system would keep as requirements input, without imposing implementation choices.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard scope item defining needs for a working two-way chat, multi-project channel system, role-based addressing, reply delivery, and monolith-to-composition interface refactoring, plus latent capabilities.
intention_for_embedding: To record the agreed scope of needs for Tim to architect and research, separating requirements from implementation decisions.
